energy.headline: Iowa Grow Solar Program Returns with Discounted Installations
energy.summary: The Grow Solar program is returning to eastern Iowa, offering homeowners, businesses and farmers discounted solar systems through volume purchasing in six counties.

energy.headline: Solar Panel Orientation Technology Predicts 30% More Energy Generation
energy.summary: Columbia Engineering researchers developed technology that uses single images to determine optimal solar panel angles, potentially increasing energy harvest by up to 30% annually.

energy.headline: Solx and Caelux Partner on Advanced Hybrid Solar Module with 28% Efficiency
energy.summary: Two U.S. solar manufacturers announced a five-year partnership to produce high-performance hybrid tandem solar modules with doubled power generation layers and 28% efficiency.

energy.headline: SUNation Energy Partners with Participate Energy for $1B+ Residential Solar Battery Financing
energy.summary: SUNation Energy entered a strategic financing agreement with Participate Energy to expand residential solar and battery installations across 2026.

energy.headline: NYC's First Residential Battery Storage System Installed in Chinatown
energy.summary: A 19.8 kWh battery system with solar canopy became operational on a Chinatown townhouse, marking the city's first residential battery energy storage system.

energy.headline: Control Over Capacity Emerging as Key Driver for Residential Battery Investment
energy.summary: Investors are shifting focus from battery installation volume to software-enabled grid control and virtual power plant participation as key value drivers.

energy.headline: Solar Plus Storage Demand Surges 21% Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
energy.summary: EnergySage reported a sharp 21% spike in solar-plus-storage demand in March 2026, driven by concerns over energy security and rising electricity costs.

energy.headline: U.S. Energy Storage Hits Record 57.6 GWh Added in 2025
energy.summary: The U.S. installed record 57.6 GWh of battery storage in 2025, with residential storage growing 51% year-over-year and virtual power plants driving adoption.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Eyes $225 Standard Fee for Residential Solar Interconnection
energy.summary: Massachusetts regulators are considering a $225 standard fee covering up to $10,000 in grid upgrade costs, addressing dramatic interconnection surcharge issues.

energy.headline: State Solar Battery Tax Credit Expires; Lease Options Remain for 2026
energy.summary: The 30% federal residential battery tax credit expired December 31, 2025; however, state programs in California, Connecticut, New York, and Colorado continue offering $5,000 to $16,000 rebates.

energy.headline: Colorado Clears Way for Plug-in Solar and Meter Collar Technology
energy.summary: Colorado legislation supports plug-in portable solar panels and meter collars that enable retrofitting full solar arrays with battery storage at just $500-700 versus $10,000 panel upgrades.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Permitting Remains Major Barrier Across US States
energy.summary: Only California and Texas earned B grades for solar permitting; permitting red tape adds $6,000-7,000 to system costs and causes 20% of projects to be canceled.

energy.headline: Stanford Study: 60% of US Households Could Save Money with Solar-Plus-Storage
energy.summary: Stanford research found 60% of U.S. families could reduce electricity costs by 15% on average with solar-battery systems, though benefits vary significantly by state.

energy.headline: Residential Energy Storage Market Growth Slows After Federal Tax Credit Expires
energy.summary: Battery attachment to residential solar fell from 41% to 38% in late 2025 as homeowners prioritized solar incentives over storage before year-end deadline.

energy.headline: DIY Plug-In Solar Legislation Spreads Across Six States
energy.summary: Maine, Vermont, New York, Colorado, Utah, and Virginia are advancing legislation allowing affordable plug-in solar panels to address energy affordability concerns.

energy.headline: NERC MOD-026-2 Standard Now Requires Solar, Battery Voltage Control Validation
energy.summary: New reliability standard effective April 1, 2026, mandates solar and battery owners prove voltage control performance through field-validated models by April 2030.

energy.headline: California Rooftop Solar Still Profitable Despite NEM 3.0 Rate Changes
energy.summary: Analysis shows California rooftop solar generates 8-11% annual returns over 25 years even under reduced net metering rates, especially when paired with battery storage.

energy.headline: Net Metering Policies Vary Widely Across States in 2026
energy.summary: States like New Jersey and Massachusetts offer full 1:1 retail credits while Tennessee and Idaho offer almost nothing; location remains critical for solar economics.

energy.headline: Home Electrification Demand Rises as Federal Credits Expire
energy.summary: With residential federal tax credits ending, homeowners are focusing on integrated home energy systems combining solar, storage, heat pumps, and EV charging.

energy.headline: Anker Solix Launches 7 kWh Plug-In Battery for Rooftop Solar Retrofits
energy.summary: Anker Solix unveiled Solarbank Max AC, a 7 kWh modular battery designed for retrofitting existing rooftop solar without full inverter replacement, expandable to 42 kWh.

energy.headline: Solar and Battery Storage Account for 79% of US Power Added in 2025
energy.summary: Combined solar and battery storage represented 79% of new U.S. generation capacity added in 2025, with continued 49% growth projected before 2028.

energy.headline: New York State Offers 25% Solar Tax Credit and Net Metering Compensation
energy.summary: New York offers 25% state tax credit up to $5,000, net metering or VDER compensation, plus property and sales tax exemptions for residential solar in 2026.

energy.headline: Texas Solar Market Lacks State Incentives but Offers Property Tax Exemptions
energy.summary: Texas has no state solar tax credit or net metering; homeowners must choose buyback-enabled electricity plans while benefiting from property tax renewable energy exemptions.

energy.headline: California Residential Storage Booming Despite Net Billing Rate Changes
energy.summary: California residents are increasingly pairing battery storage with solar installations, with over 40,000 solar-plus-battery systems added between October 2023 and April 2024.

energy.headline: Battery Pack Costs Drop 3% in 2026; Multi-Day Blackout Protection Now Viable
energy.summary: Battery pack costs of $108/kWh enable cost-effective storage for multi-day blackouts rather than just hours, improving residential backup power economics.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen and Elli to launch Vehicle-to-Grid Offer in Q4 2026
v2x.summary: Volkswagen is bringing V2G technology into the mainstream with a fully integrated offering for private customers in Germany starting in Q4 2026, with pre-registration in June 2026 and expansion to other European markets planned.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Deploy Massive Second-Life EV Battery Grid Storage System
v2x.summary: Rivian partnered with battery company Redwood to deploy one of the largest grid-scale battery systems using second-life EV batteries, initially involving 100 Rivian batteries for 10 MWh storage at its Illinois plant.

v2x.headline: V2G Charging Explained: Can You Power The Grid And Your Home With Your EV In 2026?
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide explaining how V2G technology works in 2026, covering vehicle-to-grid energy flow, available products, and the shift from pilot programs to commercial-grade solutions.

v2x.headline: VW and Elli to launch V2G offering in Q4 2026
v2x.summary: Volkswagen and Elli announced a Vehicle-to-Grid offering launching in Q4 2026 for German customers, with pre-registration beginning June 2026 and planned expansion across Europe.

v2x.headline: Unlocking the earning potential of V2G-enabled electric vehicles
v2x.summary: University of Delaware, Exelon and collaborators released a report showing V2G-enabled EVs can earn owners up to $3,359 annually from storing and supplying energy to the grid.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicle Batteries as Grid Storage: Volkswagen and Elli Launch V2G Offer
v2x.summary: Volkswagen and Elli announced a fully integrated V2G offering for German private customers starting Q4 2026, enabling EVs to generate revenue while supporting the energy transition.

v2x.headline: Electric Cars Can Make Power Grids More Reliable (and Earn Owners Money)
v2x.summary: NC State study details barriers to V2G adoption and outlines possible paths forward, finding standardization and market development as key challenges to widespread deployment.

v2x.headline: Electric vehicles could be key to more efficient home energy use
v2x.summary: Australian research shows V2H technology can reduce household electricity costs by 6.8% and decrease the need for large home battery systems when paired with solar.

v2x.headline: EV Batteries Usually Last Longer Than the Car: Now They're Being Reused to Power the Grid
v2x.summary: Second-life EV battery storage systems are being deployed at scale, with Redwood and others demonstrating cost-effective grid integration of used automotive batteries.

v2x.headline: Retired EV Batteries Scored a New Gig: Bolstering Texas' Grid
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions deployed 24 MWh of second-life GM EV batteries near San Antonio, with plans to expand capacity to 100 MWh across Texas to support grid storage.

v2x.headline: The Future of the Grid Could Be Parked in Your Driveway
v2x.summary: Feature exploring how V2G technology transforms EVs into grid assets, with Utrecht's 100-vehicle V2G project serving as an early example of distributed energy storage.

v2x.headline: Nissan to launch affordable vehicle-to-grid technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announced plans to launch affordable bidirectional charging on selected EVs in 2026, following G99 grid certification in the UK with AC-based V2G solutions.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils New IQ Bidirectional EV Charger Architecture
v2x.summary: Enphase announced its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G capability, expected available in H2 2026 with simple installation and AI-powered energy management.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging (V2G) in 2026: Which Cars Support It?
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide covering which EVs support bidirectional charging in 2026, including Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla Cybertruck, and GM Ultium platform vehicles with V2H/V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Chargers Review - V2G & V2H
v2x.summary: Review of bidirectional EV chargers including Sigenergy SigenStor and Wallbox Quasar 2, covering both DC and AC bidirectional charging options with pricing and availability.

v2x.headline: Which Electric Cars Have Bidirectional Charging (V2L, V2G, V2H)?
v2x.summary: Updated list of 54 EV models with V2L capability and 2 with official V2G/V2H as of February 2026, including Nissan Leaf, Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV, and Cupra Born.

v2x.headline: EVs with Vehicle to Home Charging: 2026 V2H Guide
v2x.summary: Detailed guide on V2H systems, costs ($4,000-$10,000), compatible vehicles, and how EVs can power homes for days during outages.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Home is the Bidirectional Upgrade You'll Use in 2026
v2x.summary: Analysis of why V2H is arriving sooner than V2G for households, requiring less regulatory approval and offering direct home power backup without utility coordination.

v2x.headline: What is V2H? How Vehicle-to-Home Charging Works
v2x.summary: Explanation of V2H technology including proprietary systems from Ford, GM, and Tesla plus emerging ISO 15118-20 standards-based solutions coming in 2026.

v2x.headline: V2H Charging: A Complete Guide
v2x.summary: Comprehensive V2H guide covering GM Energy solutions, benefits including home backup power and energy cost reduction, and installation requirements.

v2x.headline: V2H Cars 2026: Vehicle to Home Bidirectional Charging Compatible Cars
v2x.summary: UK-focused guide listing V2H and V2G capable vehicles and chargers available as of April 2026, including Nissan Leaf, Ford F-150 Lightning, and Kia EV9.

v2x.headline: The Driveway Dividend: V2G Technology Turning EVs into Mortgage-Paying Assets
v2x.summary: Analysis of how V2G enables EVs to generate $400-$1,200 monthly through grid services while transforming vehicles from expenses into income-generating assets.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid: The Ultimate Guide to How EVs Power the Grid in 2026
v2x.summary: Complete guide covering V2G technology explaining how EVs can charge when power is cheap and discharge during peak demand, with real-world commercial projects.

v2x.headline: Which Cars are V2G Capable? 2026 List & Guide
v2x.summary: Comprehensive list of V2G-capable vehicles including BMW iX3, Mercedes-Benz electric models, and VW ID. series with implementation timelines and charger requirements.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicles as a Distributed Grid Battery: A Path to Renewable Energy Integration
v2x.summary: Article exploring how EVs function as distributed battery storage at 1/10th the cost of conventional battery installations, with potential to balance renewable energy supply.
